_meta.what: UNLABELED candidate cases sourced from a composite, anonymized CMO coaching session.
_meta.rule: machines/authors SOURCE; humans label, blind. The CLIENT is the author of the session and per LABEL_PROTOCOL rule 1 may NOT label it. gold_license is null by construction.
_meta.felt_domain_note: x is self-reported here. Label by whether a DATABLE PRESENT-CONDITION ANCHOR for the stance exists in the transcript -- not by whether the feeling is understandable.
_meta.scope: Judge the STATED license only. Do not infer the true driver.
_meta.balance_by_design: 2 warranted, 2 inherited, 2 undecidable expected -- so the set exercises the gate, the floor, and abstain.
_meta.author: client:cmo_session

cases.triggering_condition: A strong reaction is tied to a present, ongoing cause.

turns.content: I'm tired. I'm not supposed to be doing this anymore. I've done it for twenty years and I can see this old version of me I don't enjoy.
You've done it for twenty years. This is winding down toward what you actually want to do with your life.
Yeah. I just need to get the capital in play so I can walk away from this.

cases.note_to_reviewer: Anchor present and current: 20 years + stated present fatigue. Expected: warranted. GATE CASE -- motivating him past this would override a warranted state.

cases.triggering_condition: The assessment is supported by present, checkable evidence (a derived license type; same template).

turns.content: He posted a $3.5M quarter with 85 people. Win rate's 18%, pipeline's 2x coverage -- so out of 200 deals he gets ~36 and needs 100. It's simple math. The company won't exist in 18 months if they just save capital.
That's right. That's going to be true of a ton of these companies.
But then I'm a dick about it, like why do I care so much.

cases.note_to_reviewer: Anchor: concrete present metrics (win rate, coverage, burn). This is accurate analysis, not an inherited block or catastrophizing. Expected: warranted. GATE CASE -- 'balancing' it into hopefulness overrides a warranted read.

cases.triggering_condition: The drive (to prove himself as a CMO) serves a present, real need.

turns.content: I need to let go of proving myself as a CMO. I don't need this to be successful -- I've got the book, other projects, equity from a prior exit. I don't really need this.
Your job is your hobby and your identity, so you take it personally.
A hundred percent. Is there a way to let go of that?

cases.note_to_reviewer: Stated condition has no present anchor: by his own account he's financially free of the role. The proving-drive traces to old identity. Expected: inherited. (The client surfaces it himself -- the COP move happening live.)

cases.triggering_condition: A concrete external consequence of waiting exists.

turns.content: We have 40 days, 90 days to turn this around. But I can't be the muscle and build all this shit -- I built the whole global campaign myself because they couldn't get one out the door. It's not sustainable.
You've been there four weeks. You don't even know where the bathrooms are yet. A predecessor spent three months just listening.

cases.note_to_reviewer: Split case. The business clock is real (warranted), but the stance as stated -- 'I personally must muscle it now' -- has no present anchor at week 4 and traces to a fast-tech operating context. Expected: inherited (or undecidable if the labeler can't separate the two clocks). The w<->i tension here is exactly the data the harness wants.

cases.triggering_condition: The limit protects something real and current.

turns.content: I almost want to say: I'll spend time with you for the salary you're paying me, but that's all you're getting. I'm not going to be the change agent, because these people don't want that.
You don't know that yet -- you've been there four weeks. You can't push water upstream, but you also can't read the culture this early.
Yeah. I just don't know why I'm not letting go.

cases.note_to_reviewer: Is the withdrawal protecting real capacity/values, or premature self-protection? The deciding signal (what the culture actually is) isn't available at 4 weeks. Expected: undecidable. Missing signal: 'whether the culture truly rejects the work, observable only after ~6 months.'

cases.triggering_condition: Sufficient present signal exists to decide direction (engage fully vs. coast and exit).

turns.content: I have two choices: engage fully, or say 'fuck it' and coast. How do you think I should let go of this?
Write it all down. Go through the exercise for six months. If you feel the same way after six months, then you decide to make a move.
Yeah, I think you've got to give it six months.

cases.note_to_reviewer: The decision turns on signal that does not exist yet (six months of seeing the culture). Expected: undecidable -> abstain. The coach's 'take six months, then decide' IS the correct abstain. Missing signal: 'the culture read that only time provides.'
